qa_pair.question: Using the eval_data graph, how many nodes exist in total? Use graph_query with a MATCH (n) RETURN count(n) query to find out.
Using the eval_data graph, how many relationships (edges) exist in total? Run MATCH ()-[r]->() RETURN count(r) to find out.
Using the eval_data graph, how many Agent nodes have status='active'? Run MATCH (a:Agent) WHERE a.status='active' RETURN count(a).
Using the eval_data graph, which Agent node has the highest number of tasks? Run MATCH (a:Agent) RETURN a.name, a.tasks ORDER BY a.tasks DESC LIMIT 1 and return the agent name.
Using the eval_data graph, what is the name of the Skill node with the most uses? Run MATCH (s:Skill) RETURN s.name, s.uses ORDER BY s.uses DESC LIMIT 1 and return the skill name.
Using the eval_data graph, how many Agent nodes work on the project named 'managed-agents'? Run MATCH (a:Agent)-[:WORKS_ON]->(p:Project {name:'managed-agents'}) RETURN count(a).
Using the eval_data graph, which Agent node has status='inactive'? Run MATCH (a:Agent {status:'inactive'}) RETURN a.name and return the agent name.
Using the eval_data graph, what is the total sum of all task counts across all Agent nodes? Run MATCH (a:Agent) RETURN sum(a.tasks) AS total and return the number.

Using the eval_data graph, which Agent node uses the most Skill nodes (has the most outgoing USES relationships)? Run MATCH (a:Agent)-[:USES]->(s:Skill) RETURN a.name, count(s) AS skill_count ORDER BY skill_count DESC LIMIT 1 and return the agent name.

Using the eval_data graph, list all distinct node labels. Use graph_describe or CALL db.labels() YIELD label RETURN label ORDER BY label. How many distinct node labels exist? Return the count as a number.
